posts.author: hardik_shah1
posts.community: Greaves Cotton - Is this up to investors?
posts.text: Just look at the quality of products of the top 4-5 companies and the next rung. The bet should be on who wins when EV becomes the preferred choice. Just like ICE, where there are 4 players at the mass market and another 3 at the Premium end, same would happen in EVs ( Here the mass market would mean Ather, TVS and Bajaj and premium being ultraviolette etc.

posts.author: kumarpalj
posts.community: Greaves Cotton - Is this up to investors?
posts.text: Thanks for sharing the data. One interesting observation - I see is Hero is gaining a significant market share here. Hero also has a 30% stake in Ather as well.

posts.author: Aloysius_De_Sa
posts.community: Greaves Cotton - Is this up to investors?
posts.text: What makes the opportunity so exciting is that it seems the market completely ignores Ampere in the market cap… infact it could as well be having a negative valuation. While Genset makers are commanding high valuations due to shortage supply and AI story, Greaves Cotton has not really played the story and has just come to par after a horrid March. We see how good EV stories like Ather are performing and sooner or later, I feel the market will take note. They should be looking towards more export opportunities and the management seems genuine.

posts.author: CoffeeEngineer
posts.community: Greaves Cotton - Is this up to investors?
posts.text: I was bored and saw this link https://www.ankmitra.com/vahan-2w-ev-daily-sales-data kudos to @hardik_shah1 in Ola Electric post. Saw some interesting growth in Greaves so decided to run some numbers. Greaves 2W EV Sales should be around 25k units for Q2(extrapolated June data from run rate till now). That’s up 38% QoQ and 97% YoY Then I noticed EV sales in general have increased since March(coinciding with the war in Iran). Maybe because of petrol price increase? but that can’t be it because prices were increased on 15th May. One factor that can be the reason is shortage of petrol(i don’t know…
